CREATE TABLE timestamps (id INT, ts1 TIMESTAMPTZ, ts2 TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-20 20:38:48', TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-03-22 01:02:03.1234');
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '1995-05-15 15:30:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '1994-10-10 12:00:00');
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (3, TIMESTAMPTZ '2020-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '2020-01-01 23:59:59');
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (4, TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-01-01 00:00:01');
SELECT id, greatest(ts1, ts2) AS latest_timestamp FROM timestamps;
CREATE TABLE additional_timestamps (id INT, ts TIMESTAMPTZ);
SELECT a.id, greatest(a.ts, b.ts1) AS latest_timestamp FROM additional_timestamps a JOIN timestamps b ON a.id = 1;
SELECT id, greatest(TIMESTAMPTZ '1999-12-31 23:59:59', TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-01-01 00:00:00') AS boundary_test;
SELECT id, greatest(ts1, TIMESTAMPTZ '2023-04-07 15:00:00') AS comparison_from_subquery FROM (SELECT ts1 FROM timestamps WHERE id = 2) AS ts_subquery;
WITH timestamp_pairs AS (SELECT ts1, ts2 FROM timestamps) SELECT greatest(ts1, ts2) FROM timestamp_pairs;
SELECT id, greatest(ts1, ts2) AS latest FROM timestamps WHERE greatest(ts1, ts2) > TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-21 00:00:00';
SELECT COUNT(*) AS greater_count FROM timestamps WHERE greatest(ts1, ts2) = ts2;
